Let $(a_1, b_1), \dots, (a_n, b_n)$ be $n$ points in $\mathbb{R}^2$ (where
$\mathbb{R}$ denotes the real numbers), and let $\varepsilon > 0$ be a positive
number. Can we find a real-valued function $f(x, y)$ that satisfies the
following three conditions?

\begin{enumerate}[(a)]
\item $f(0, 0) = 1$;
\item $f(x, y) \ne 0$ for only finitely many $(x, y) \in \mathbb{R}^2$;
\item $\displaystyle \sum_{r=1}^n |f(x + a_r, y + b_r) - f(x, y)| < \varepsilon$ for every $(x, y) \in \mathbb{R}^2$.
\end{enumerate}
Justify your answer.
